document: MAS Notice 648 Issuance of Covered Bonds by Banks Incorporated in Singapore 20240516 (Singapore - Row 13)

sections.heading: Only a bank incorporated in Singapore may issue covered bonds in Singapore. A bank
sections.text:
2 
Only a bank incorporated in Singapore may issue covered bonds in Singapore. A bank 
incorporated outside Singapore must not issue any covered bonds through its branch in 
Singapore.

sections.heading: MAS Notice 628 on Securitisation shall not apply to a bank incorporated in Singapore
sections.text:
3 
MAS Notice 628 on Securitisation shall not apply to a bank incorporated in Singapore 
in relation to its issuance of covered bonds in accordance with the requirements in this Notice. 
For the avoidance of doubt, all requirements of MAS Notice 628 shall continue to apply to such 
a bank incorporated in Singapore in respect of its other securitisation activities. 
 
Definitions

sections.heading: In this Notice—
sections.text:
4 
In this Notice— 
 
"covered bonds" means any bonds, notes or other debentures issued by a bank or an 
SPV where payment of the liabilities to the holders of such covered bonds and any 
liabilities arising from the enforcement of the rights of the holders of the covered bonds 
are: 
 
(a) secured by a cover pool; and 
 
(b) recoverable from the bank regardless of whether the cover pool is sufficient to pay 
off such liabilities; 
 
 
 
"cover pool" in relation to an issuance of covered bonds, means a pool of assets that 
are –

(a) legally or beneficially owned or legally and beneficially owned by the bank or an 
SPV, 
 
(b) held by the bank as trustee, or a replacement trustee, on behalf of an SPV, or 
 
(c) both, 
 
for the purposes of securing the payment of: 
 
(i) the liabilities to the holders of the covered bonds; 
 
(ii) any liabilities arising from the enforcement of the rights of the holders of the 
covered bonds; and 
 
(iii) any liabilities to third party service providers appointed for the operation and 
administration of the covered bond programme; 
 
 
"MAS Bills" means any securities issued by the Monetary Authority of Singapore under 
the Monetary Authority of Singapore Act 1970 ; 
 
 
"Memorandum of Compliance" means a memorandum prepared by the bank setting 
out how it has complied with each of the requirements in paragraphs 6(a) to (i), and 
8(a) of this Notice; 
 
 
"Singapore Government Securities" means any debt securities issued by the 
Singapore Government under any written law; and 
[MAS Notice 648 (Amendment) 2021] 
 
 
"SPV" means any special purpose vehicle incorporated or established in Singapore for 
the primary purpose of issuing covered bonds or holding the cover pool in relation to 
such covered bonds or both.

sections.heading: The expressions used in this Notice shall, except where expressly defined in this Notice
sections.text:
5 
The expressions used in this Notice shall, except where expressly defined in this Notice 
or where the context otherwise requires, have the same respective meanings as in the Banking 
Act 1970.

Cover Pool Assets and Encumbrance Limit 
 

A bank incorporated in Singapore must, when issuing covered bonds itself or through 
an SPV, comply with or ensure that the SPV complies with, as the case may be, the following 
requirements at all times: 
 
(a) the cover pool must comprise mortgage loans secured by residential property 
("residential mortgage loans"), whether in Singapore or elsewhere, and may also 
comprise one or more of the following assets: 
 
(i) any other loans secured by the same residential property as the residential 
mortgage loans; 
 
(ii) assets including intangible properties that form part of all the security provided 
for the residential mortgage loans such as guarantees and indemnities; 
 
(iii) any interest held by the bank as trustee or a replacement trustee for the SPV 
in relation to the residential mortgage loans or the assets referred to in 
paragraph 6(a)(i) to (ii); 
 
(iv) derivatives held for the purposes of hedging risks arising from the particular 
issuance of covered bonds; 
 
(v) cash (including foreign currency); 
 
(vi) Singapore Government Securities; and 
 
(vii) MAS Bills; 
 
 
(b) the aggregate value of assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(v) to (vii) must not 
exceed 15% of the aggregate value of the residential mortgage loans and the 
assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(i) to (vii) included in the cover pool; 
 
(ba) notwithstanding sub-paragraph (b), where – 
(i) the payment obligations to holders of the covered bonds by the bank or SPV, 
as the case may be, under the terms and conditions relating to an issuance of 
covered bonds ("relevant payment obligations") are or will be due and payable 
within the next 12 months by the bank or SPV, as the case may be, the 
aggregate value of assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(v) to (vii) may exceed 
15% of the aggregate value of the residential mortgage loans and the assets 
referred to in paragraph 6(a)(i) to (vii) included in the cover pool up to an 
amount not more than the total amount of the relevant payment obligations in 
that 12 month period; or

(ii) there are differences between the time of receipt and use of the assets 
referred to in paragraph 6(a)(v) to (vii) in the cover pool by the bank or SPV, 
as the case may be, the aggregate value of assets referred to in paragraph 
6(a)(v) to (vii) may exceed 15% of the aggregate value of the residential 
mortgage loans and the assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(i) to (vii) for a 
period not exceeding one month from the date on which the bank or SPV, as 
the case may be, has receipt of the assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(v) to 
(vii); 
 
 
(c) the aggregate value of – 
 
(i) the residential mortgage loans and the assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(i) 
to (vii) included in cover pools for all covered bonds issued by the bank, SPV, 
or both the bank and SPV, as the case may be; and 
 
(ii) the residential mortgage loans and the assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(i) 
to (vii), not falling within sub-paragraph (i) transferred by the bank to the SPV, 
 
must not exceed 10% of the value of the total assets of the bank1 at all times. For 
the purposes of determining: 
 
(A) the aggregate value of assets in the cover pools, the bank or SPV, as the case 
may be, must include assets used as collateral above an amount equal to 103% 
of the outstanding nominal amount of the covered bonds secured by those 
assets; and 
 
(B) the total assets of the bank, the bank must exclude assets which the bank 
uses to meet regulatory requirements under sections 382, 39 and 40, read with 
section 65, of the Banking Act 1970, section 8 of the Deposit Insurance and 
Policy Owners' Protection Schemes Act 2011 and other regulatory 
requirements as may be prescribed or specified by the Authority; 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
1 Total assets of the bank include assets of the branches but does not include assets of the subsidiaries of the 
bank. 
 
2 Deleted [MAS Notice 648 (Amendment) 2020]

(d) the bank must continue to hold capital against its exposures in respect of the 
assets included in a cover pool in accordance with MAS Notice 637 on Risk Based 
Capital Adequacy Requirements for Banks Incorporated in Singapore ("MAS 
Notice 637"). In the case where the bank uses an SPV to issue covered bonds or 
where the cover pool is held by an SPV, the bank must apply a "look through" 
approach for the purposes of computing its risk-based capital requirements under 
MAS Notice 637. Under the "look through" approach, the bank and the SPV must 
be treated as a single entity for the purposes of MAS Notice 6373; 
 
 
(e) deleted 
 
 
(f) for residential mortgage loans included in the cover pool, the bank or SPV, as the 
case may be, must conduct on an annual basis at the minimum, a valuation of 
residential properties used to secure the loans. The bank or SPV, as the case may 
be, must conduct the valuation on a more frequent basis where the property market 
is subject to significant changes in conditions; 
 
 
(g) where a residential mortgage loan included in the cover pool is secured by a 
residential property located in a foreign jurisdiction, the bank or SPV, as the case 
may be, must seek legal advice to confirm that the laws of that foreign jurisdiction 
do not adversely affect the rights of a covered bond holder, the cover pool monitor, 
a liquidator, a receiver or any trustee (if any) appointed to hold the cover pool, over 
the residential property; 
 
 
(h) the bank or SPV, as the case may be, must ensure that the aggregate value of 
assets in a cover pool is at least 103% of the outstanding nominal amount of the 
covered bonds secured by the assets at all times. For the purposes of this sub- 
paragraph, the value of each residential mortgage loan included in the cover pool, 
and of the assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(i) secured on the same residential 
property as the residential mortgage loan and forming part of the cover pool 
(collectively for the purposes of this sub-paragraph, the "loans"), shall be – 
 
(i) where the aggregate value of the loans is more than 80% of the valuation 
(conducted in accordance with paragraph 6(f)) of the residential property that 
is used to secure the loans, 80% of the current market valuation of that 
residential property; or 
 
 
 
 
 
3  Deleted [MAS Notice 648 (Amendment) 2024]

(ii) where the aggregate value of the loans is not more than 80% of the valuation 
(conducted in accordance with paragraph 6(f)) of the residential property that 
is used to secure the loans, the aggregate value of the loans; and4 
 
 
(i) the bank or SPV, as the case may be, must keep a register containing such details 
as is necessary to clearly identify all the assets in a cover pool.

sections.heading: For the purposes of determining the total assets of the bank under paragraph 6(c)(B) —
sections.text:
6A 
For the purposes of determining the total assets of the bank under paragraph 6(c)(B) — 
 
(a) 
the bank incorporated in Singapore must comply with all of the following: 
 
(i) 
the bank must compute the assets which the bank uses to meet the 
regulatory requirements under section 38 of the Banking Act 1970 
("relevant assets"), without applying any haircuts specified in MAS Notice 
649; and 
 
(ii) 
the bank must compute the assets which the bank uses to meet the 
regulatory requirements under section 40 of the Banking Act 1970, 
without applying any adjustment factors specified in any notice in writing 
issued under section 40(1) of the Banking Act  1970; and 
 
(b) 
the bank incorporated in Singapore may do all of the following: 
 
(i) 
where the bank holds assets in excess of that which the bank is required 
to hold to meet the regulatory requirements mentioned in paragraph 
6(c)(B), the bank need only exclude the minimum amount of assets the 
bank is required to hold to meet the regulatory requirements mentioned 
in paragraph 6(c)(B); 
 
(ii) 
where the bank holds more than one type of asset which can be used to 
meet the regulatory requirements under section 38 of the Banking Act 
1970, for the purposes of computing the relevant assets, the bank may 
exclude assets with the lowest haircuts first, followed by assets with the 
next lowest haircut, and so on; and 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
4 For the avoidance of doubt, a bank incorporated in Singapore must continue to comply with the loan-to-value 
limits set out in MAS Notice 632 on Residential Property Loans for the purposes of granting any credit facility 
for the purchase of residential property or any credit facility otherwise secured by residential property.

(iii) 
where a bank holds more than one type of asset which can be used to 
meet the regulatory requirements under section 40 of the Banking Act 
1970, for the purposes of computing the assets which the bank uses to 
meet the regulatory requirements under section 40 of the Banking Act 
1970, the bank may exclude assets with the highest adjustment factor 
first, followed by assets with the next highest adjustment factor, and so 
on. 
 
 
 
6B   For the purposes of determining the total assets of the bank under paragraph 6(c)(B), the 
bank must compute the relevant assets used to meet the requirements of MAS Notice 649 as 
follows: 
 
(a) 
in the case of a bank referred to in paragraph 24 of MAS Notice 649, in either 
of the following ways: 
 
(i) 
as the amount of assets which the bank uses to comply with MAS Notice 
649 on a consolidated level as required under paragraph 25 of MAS 
Notice 649; or 
 
(ii) 
as the amount of assets which the bank would have used to comply with 
MAS Notice 649, if the bank – 
 
(A) 
consolidates the assets and liabilities of all its branches, but not 
of its other banking group entities (as defined in MAS Notice 
649); and 
 
(B) 
deems all assets, liabilities, transactions, exposures or 
operations of its branches, but not of its other banking group 
entities (as defined in MAS Notice 649), to be that of the bank 
for the purposes of Part II of MAS Notice 649 (other than 
paragraph 25 of MAS Notice 649), 
 
notwithstanding anything to the contrary in paragraphs 25 and 26 of 
MAS Notice 649, 
 
and the bank must comply with paragraphs 6C and 6D as applicable; 
 
[MAS Notice 648 (Amendment) 2022] 
 
(b) 
in the case of a bank not referred to in paragraph 24 of MAS Notice 649 and 
which has obtained the Authority's approval under paragraph 6 of MAS Notice 
649 to comply with the requirements set out in MAS Notice 649 on a country- 
level group basis ("relevant approval"), in either of the following ways: 
 
(i) 
as the amount of assets which the bank uses to comply with MAS Notice 
649 on a country-level group basis; or 
 
(ii) 
as the amount of assets which the bank would have used to comply with 
MAS Notice 649 if the bank had not obtained the relevant approval, 
 
and the bank must comply with paragraphs 6C and 6D as applicable; and

[MAS Notice 648 (Amendment) 2022] 
 
(c) 
in the case of a bank not referred to in paragraph 24 of MAS Notice 649 and 
which has not obtained the relevant approval, as the amount of assets which 
the bank uses to comply with MAS Notice 649. 
 
[MAS Notice 648 (Amendment) 2022] 
 
 
6C A bank that wishes to use the computation method in paragraph 6B(a)(ii) or paragraph 
6B(b)(ii) must notify the Authority at least three business days prior to using the computation 
method. 
 
 
6D Where the bank has provided notification to the Authority in accordance with paragraph 6C 
and the bank subsequently wishes to compute the relevant assets in accordance with 
paragraph 6B(a)(i) or paragraph 6B(b)(i), the bank must notify the Authority at least three 
business days prior to changing the method of computation.

sections.heading: A bank incorporated in Singapore which issues covered bonds must only use its own
sections.text:
7 
A bank incorporated in Singapore which issues covered bonds must only use its own 
assets, and not that of other entities in its banking group (as defined in MAS Notice 637) to 
form the cover pool and must ensure that every of its subsidiary which intends to issue covered 
bonds only uses the subsidiary's own assets, and not that of other entities in the banking group 
to form the cover pool. 
 
 
Risk Management Requirements

sections.heading: A bank incorporated in Singapore must, when issuing covered bonds itself or through
sections.text:
8 
A bank incorporated in Singapore must, when issuing covered bonds itself or through 
an SPV, comply or ensure that the SPV complies with, as the case may be, the following 
conditions to manage the risks arising from the issuance of covered bonds: 
 
(a) the bank or SPV, as the case may be, must have in place appropriate governance 
arrangements (such as identifying the approval authority within the bank or SPV, 
where the bank uses an SPV to issue covered bonds, with respect to the covered 
bond programme), performing regular and in any case, on an annual basis at the 
minimum, asset coverage tests ("ACTs") to ensure collateral quality and the proper 
level of over-collateralisation, and conducting regular stress tests on risks arising 
from issuing covered bonds such as default, prepayment, currency, interest rate, 
counterparty and liquidity risks. The bank or SPV must ensure that its board and 
senior management or trustee, as the case may be, are responsible for conducting 
due diligence in assessing the risks associated with issuing covered bonds and 
ensuring that risk management processes that are put in place for covered bonds 
are adhered to;

(b) the bank or SPV, as the case may be, must appoint an external third party, qualified 
to be an auditor under the Companies Act 19675, as the cover pool monitor to: 
 
(i) 
verify annually that the bank or SPV, as the case may be, has complied with 
paragraphs 6(a) to (i), 6A, 6B, 6C and 6D; 
 
(ii) verify that the bank or SPV, as the case may be, keeps an accurate register 
of the assets in the cover pool referred to in paragraph 6(i); 
 
(iii) assess the adequacy of the bank's or SPV's, as the case may be, risk 
management process and internal controls relating to the covered bond 
programme annually, including an independent review of ACTs performed by 
the bank or SPV, as the case may be; 
 
(iv) submit a certified report to the Authority annually on paragraph 8(b)(i) to (iii) 
no later than the last day of the third month immediately following the end of 
the bank's financial year; and 
 
 
(v) report to the Authority immediately if it becomes aware that the bank or SPV, 
as the case may be, has breached any of the conditions imposed; 
 
 
 
(c) the bank must obtain a legal opinion to confirm that assets included in the cover 
pool are beyond its and its creditors' reach, even in an insolvency situation; 
 
 
(d) where a bank transfers the legal right to, or perfects the assignment of, the assets 
comprising the cover pool, the bank must disclose (together with any form of notice 
necessary for such transfer or perfection) to each borrower whose residential 
mortgage loan or asset referred to in paragraph 6(a)(i) to (iii) to which the transfer 
or assignment applies, the consequences of such transfer or perfection of 
assignment, as the case may be, including whether the borrowers have a right to 
set-off their residential mortgage loans or loans, as the case may be, against any 
deposits that they have placed with the bank; and 
 
 
(e) the bank or SPV, as the case may be, must disclose to the covered bond holders, 
results of ACTs performed and cover pool characteristics on a regular basis and 
in any event, at least every quarter. 
 
 
 
 
5 The external auditor of the bank can concurrently act as the bank's cover pool monitor, if the bank assesses that 
there is no conflict of interest.

Notification and Other Requirements 
 

A bank incorporated in Singapore must furnish to the Authority at least one month prior 
to the issuance of covered bonds, information on its covered bond programme in writing 
including the type of assets that will be included in the cover pool under the covered bond 
programme and sign-off from its board and senior management on the covered bond 
programme6. In addition, the bank must notify the Authority in writing at least three business 
days prior to the issuance of any covered bonds under the covered bond programme submitted 
to the Authority. The bank must provide at the minimum, information on the size, tenure and 
terms of the issuance, and the amount of assets used to back the covered bonds. The bank 
must also at the same time submit a Memorandum of Compliance to the Authority. The bank 
must submit any additional information, as requested by the Authority, relating to its application 
and issuance of the covered bonds.

sections.heading: This Notice shall take immediate effect.
sections.text:
11 
This Notice shall take immediate effect. 
 
*Notes on History of Amendments 
1. MAS Notice 648 (Amendment) 2015 with effect from 4 June 2015. 
2. MAS Notice 648 (Amendment) 2020 with effect from 16 October 2020. 
3. MAS Notice 648 (Amendment) 2021 with effect from 27 September 2021. 
4. MAS Notice 648 (Amendment) 2022 with effect from 1 July 2022. 
5. MAS Notice 648 (Amendment) 2024 with effect from 16 May 2024. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
6 The bank must notify the Authority in writing if there are changes to the covered bond programme.
